Form 1040 line 33 represents total payments.  
Form 1040 line 24 represents total tax.

If total tax exceeds total payments, you owe the difference, which is reported on line 37.

36267354c8e82c9b8490035ba0d8b65f02578ce6:
Head of household applies to an unmarried taxpayer.  
An unmarried taxpayer must have paid more than half the cost of keeping up a home for a qualifying person.  
A qualifying person can be a dependent child.  
The head of household status must be maintained for more than half the year.  
Head of household status provides a larger standard deduction than Single status.  
Head of household status offers wider tax brackets than Single status.
36652359ca21f2ed36bbfe495169039c6dcc611d:
W-2 box 2 reports federal income tax already withheld from the employee's pay.  
W-2 box 2 is entered on Form 1040 line 25a.  
W-2 box 2 counts as a payment toward the year's tax.
37ea314274a5c72df06059730af34bcba549232e:
Form 1040 line 33 represents total payments made.  
Form 1040 line 24 indicates total tax owed.  
If total payments exceed total tax, the difference is your refund, which is reported on line 34.  
If total tax exceeds total payments, you owe the difference, which is reported on line 37.
39697f89497b49e087c852693abbca1556839652: A taxpayer who is age 65 or older gets an additional standard deduction on top of the basic amount. A taxpayer who is blind gets an additional standard deduction on top of the basic amount. A taxpayer who is both age 65 or older and blind receives the additional standard deduction twice. Most filers who are under 65 and not blind use only the basic standard deduction.
3b0ef84cf43e6b7216d62a17f5a8218c687bcff7:
The TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married individuals filing separately start at 10% for income up to $11,925.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married individuals filing separately are 12% for income between $11,925 and $48,475.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married individuals filing separately are 22% for income between $48,475 and $103,350.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married individuals filing separately are 24% for income between $103,350 and $197,300.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married individuals filing separately are 32% for income between $197,300 and $250,525.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married individuals filing separately are 35% for income between $250,525 and $375,800.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married individuals filing separately are 37% for income above $375,800.  
The thresholds for married individuals filing separately match those for single filers except in the top two tax brackets.
3b8d2ab8277cb0168a03a1b96aea9febea185a0d:
TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married filing jointly start at 10% for income up to $23,850.  
The 12% tax bracket for married filing jointly applies to income between $23,850 and $96,950.  
The 22% tax bracket for married filing jointly applies to income between $96,950 and $206,700.  
The 24% tax bracket for married filing jointly applies to income between $206,700 and $394,600.  
The 32% tax bracket for married filing jointly applies to income between $394,600 and $501,050.  
The 35% tax bracket for married filing jointly applies to income between $501,050 and $751,600.  
The 37% tax bracket for married filing jointly applies to income above $751,600.
3d8ddd1cf0b7569b6f94c298f4da2c32da5afce7:
Form 1040 line 11 represents adjusted gross income (AGI).  
Adjusted gross income (AGI) is calculated as total income (line 9) minus adjustments to income from Schedule 1.  
If there are only W-2 wages and no adjustments, adjusted gross income (AGI) equals total income.

The TY2025 ordinary income tax bracket for married filing jointly is 10% for income up to $23,850.

46d76149175642160cea72f87e55eeb879e88729:
TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for single filers include a 10% rate for income up to $11,925.  
The 12% tax rate applies to income between $11,925 and $48,475 for single filers.  
Single filers are taxed at a 22% rate on income between $48,475 and $103,350.  
The 24% tax rate for single filers applies to income between $103,350 and $197,300.  
Single filers face a 32% tax rate on income between $197,300 and $250,525.  
The 35% tax rate for single filers applies to income between $250,525 and $626,350.  
Single filers are taxed at a 37% rate on income above $626,350.
4991e2ed6686a9b8ae49603ca4a3929c30591619: A taxpayer under 65 generally must file a 2025 federal return if their gross income is at least the standard deduction for their filing status. 50f94cc553cebfb6dacd0d7b2213fbd0a0105c39: A taxpayer claims the LARGER of the standard deduction or total itemized deductions on Form 1040 line 12. Most W-2 wage earners take the standard deduction. W-2 wage earners typically find their itemized deductions to be smaller than the standard deduction.
5749721c88b626bc899afa63c476bf8bafd76384:
Form 1040 (Simplified) is used for filing a U.S. individual income tax return for tax year 2025.  
The guide provides instructions for wage earners whose only income is from W-2 jobs.  
Line 1a requires entering the amount from Box 1 of the W-2, which includes wages, tips, and other compensation.  
If there are multiple W-2s, the amounts in Box 1 should be added together.  
Line 9 represents total income, which equals Line 1a for returns with only W-2 wages.  
Line 11 indicates that adjusted gross income (AGI) equals Line 9 if there are no above-the-line adjustments.  
Line 12 specifies the standard deduction amounts for tax year 2025 based on filing status.  
The standard deduction for single filers is $15,750 for tax year 2025.  
The standard deduction for married filing jointly is $31,500 for tax year 2025.  
The standard deduction for head of household is $23,625 for tax year 2025.  
The standard deduction for married filing separately is $15,750 for tax year 2025.  
Line 15 calculates taxable income by subtracting Line 12 from Line 11, with a minimum of zero.  
Line 16 requires computing tax on taxable income using the 2025 tax-rate schedule for the filing status.  
The 2025 tax brackets for single filers and married filing separately start at 10% for income from $0 to $11,925.  
The 2025 tax brackets for single filers and married filing separately apply 12% on income over $11,925 up to $48,475.  
The 2025 tax brackets for single filers and married filing separately apply 22% on income over $48,475 up to $103,350.  
The 2025 tax brackets for single filers and married filing separately apply 24% on income over $103,350 up to $197,300.  
The 2025 tax brackets for married filing jointly start at 10% for income from $0 to $23,850.  
The 2025 tax brackets for married filing jointly apply 12% on income over $23,850 up to $96,950.  
The 2025 tax brackets for married filing jointly apply 22% on income over $96,950 up to $206,700.  
The 2025 tax brackets for married filing jointly apply 24% on income over $206,700 up to $394,600.  
The 2025 tax brackets for head of household start at 10% for income from $0 to $17,000.  
The 2025 tax brackets for head of household apply 12% on income over $17,000 up to $64,850.  
The 2025 tax brackets for head of household apply 22% on income over $64,850 up to $103,350.  
The 2025 tax brackets for head of household apply 24% on income over $103,350 up to $197,300.  
Line 25 requires entering the amount from Box 2 of the W-2, which indicates federal income tax withheld.  
Line 33 represents total payments, which equals Line 25 for a wage-only return.  
Line 34 calculates overpayment or refund by subtracting Line 16 from Line 33 if total payments exceed tax.  
Line 37 indicates the amount owed if Line 16 exceeds Line 33, calculated by subtracting Line 33 from Line 16.  
A completed return must clearly state filing status, total income, AGI, standard deduction taken, taxable income, computed tax, total federal tax withheld, and final refund or amount owed.
5abf66a5266d0ecbb8848d8045aa7f6ce31c07ef:
Form 1040 line 24 represents total tax.  
Form 1040 line 25a indicates federal income tax withheld from W-2 box 2.  
Form 1040 line 33 shows total payments, which include withholding plus any credits and estimated payments.

The standard deduction for Single filers in TY2025 is $15,750.

The standard deduction for Head of household in TY2025 is $23,625.  
The standard deduction amounts should be entered on Form 1040 line 12.
5e2bd3dadf64e156d82d8338ce959912beb0e446:
Line 1a of Form 1040 represents total W-2 box 1 wages.  
Line 9 of Form 1040 indicates total income.  
Line 11 of Form 1040 shows adjusted gross income (AGI).  
Line 12 of Form 1040 is the standard deduction.  
Line 15 of Form 1040 calculates taxable income as line 11 minus line 12, not below zero.  
Line 16 of Form 1040 computes tax on taxable income.  
Lines 22 and 24 of Form 1040 represent total tax.  
Line 25a of Form 1040 reflects federal income tax withheld from W-2 box 2.  
Line 33 of Form 1040 totals payments.  
If line 33 is greater than line 24, line 34 of Form 1040 indicates the refund; otherwise, line 37 shows the amount owed.

f091343665e4d4ed1b11c01537d4647b4ebdf830:
Single filing status applies to a taxpayer who is unmarried on the last day of the tax year.  
Single filing status applies to a taxpayer who is divorced on the last day of the tax year.  
Single filing status applies to a taxpayer who is legally separated on the last day of the tax year.  
A taxpayer with single filing status does not qualify for head of household.
fc7dceb39609af18b294065544d06234b76dd6d6:
Single filing status applies to a taxpayer who is unmarried on the last day of the tax year.  
Single filing status applies to a taxpayer who is divorced on the last day of the tax year.  
Single filing status applies to a taxpayer who is legally separated on the last day of the tax year.  
A taxpayer must not qualify for head of household to use single filing status.
